verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Simon Peter said unto Him, Lord, whither goest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered him, Whither I go thou canst not follow Me now, but thou shalt follow Me afterwards.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A great thing is love, and stronger than fire itself, and it goeth up to the very heaven; there is no hindrance which can restrain its tearing force.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so the most fervent Peter, when he hears, “Whither I go ye cannot come,” what saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Lord, whither goest thou?” and this he said, not so much from wish to learn, as from desire to follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To say openly, “I go,” he dared not yet, but, “Whither goest thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ answered, not to his words, but to his thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that this was his wish, is clear from what Christ said, “Whither I go thou canst not follow Me now.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that he longed for the following Him, and therefore asked the question?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when he heard, “thou shalt follow Me afterwards,” not even so did he restrain his longing, and, though he had gained good hopes, he is so eager as to say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why cannot I follow Thee now?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I will lay down my life for Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he had shaken off the dread of being the traitor, and was shown to be one of His own, he afterwards asked boldly himself, while the others held their peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sayest thou, Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said, ‘thou canst not,’ and thou sayest, ‘I can’?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore thou shalt know from this temptation that thy love is nothing without the presence of the impulse from above.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence it is clear that in care for him He allowed even that fall.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He desired indeed to teach him even by the first words, but when he continued in his vehemence, He did not indeed throw or force him into the denial, but left him alone, that he might learn his own weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ had said that He must be betrayed; Peter replied, “Be it far from Thee, Lord; this shall not happen unto Thee.” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was rebuked, but not instructed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On the contrary, when Christ desired to wash his feet, he said, “Thou shalt never wash my feet.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, when he hears, “Thou canst not follow Me now,” he saith, “Though all deny Thee, I will not deny Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then it was likely that he would be lifted up to folly by his practice of contradiction, Jesus next teacheth him not to oppose Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This too Luke implies, when he telleth us that Christ said, “And I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not” ( Luke xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 32 ); that is, “that thou be not finally lost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In every way teaching him humility, and proving that human nature by itself is nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, since great love made him apt for contradiction, He now sobereth him, that he might not in after times be subject to this, when he should have received the stewardship of the world, but remembering what he had suffered, might know himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And look at the violence of his fall; it did not happen to him once or twice, but he was so beside himself, that in a short time thrice did he utter the words of denial, that he might learn that he did not so love as he was loved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet, to one who had so fallen He saith again, “Lovest thou Me more than these?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that the denial was caused not by the cooling of his love, but from his having been stripped of aid from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He accepteth then Peter’s love, but cutteth off the spirit of contradiction engendered by it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if thou lovest, thou oughtest to obey Him who is beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I said to thee and to those with thee, ‘Thou canst not’; why art thou contentious?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowest thou what a thing it is to contradict God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since thou wilt not learn in this way that it is impossible that what I say should not come to pass, thou shalt learn it in the denial.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet this appeared to thee to be much more incredible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this thou did not even understand, but of that thou hadst the knowledge in thy heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet still that came to pass which was not even expected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I will lay down my life for Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since he had heard, “Greater love than this hath, no man,” he straightway sprang forward, insatiably eager and desirous to reach even to the highest pitch of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Christ, to show that it belonged to Himself alone to promise these things with authority, saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Before the cock crow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “now”; there was but a little interval.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He spake when it was late at night, and the first and second watch was past.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Chap. xiv. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let not your heart be troubled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He saith, because it was probable that when they heard they would be troubled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the leader of their band, one so entirely fervent, was told that before the cock crew he should thrice deny his Master, it was likely that they would expect to have to undergo some great reverse, sufficient to bend even souls of adamant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then it was probable that they considering these things would be astounded, see how He comforteth them, saying, “Let not your heart be troubled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this first word showing the power of His Godhead, because, what they had in their hearts He knew and brought to light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye believe in God, believe also in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “All dangers shall pass you by, for faith in Me and in My Father is more powerful than the things which come upon you, and will permit no evil thing to prevail against you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In My Father’s house are many mansions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As He comforteth Peter when bewildered by saying, “but thou shalt follow afterwards,” so also He gives this glimpse of hope to the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For lest they should think that the promise was given to him alone, He saith, “In My Father’s house are many mansions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If it were not so I would have said to you, I go to prepare a place for you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “The same place which receiveth Peter shall receive you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a great abundance of dwellings is there, and it may not be said that they need preparation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He said, “Ye cannot follow Me now,” that they might not deem that they were finally cut off, He added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That where I am, there ye may be also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So earnest have I been concerning this matter, that I should already have been given up to it, had not preparation been made long ago for you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing them that they ought to be very bold and confident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then that He may not seem to speak as though enticing them, but that they may believe the thing to be so, He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He giveth them proof that these things were not said without a meaning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He used these words, because He knew in Himself that their souls now desired to learn this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Peter said what he said, not in order to learn, but that he might follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when Peter had been rebuked, and Christ had declared that to be possible which for the time seemed impossible, and when the apparent impossibility led him to desire to know the matter exactly, therefore He saith to the others, “And the way ye know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as when He hath said, “Thou shalt deny Me,” before any one spake a word, searching into their hearts, He said, “Be not troubled,” so here also by saying “Ye know,” He disclosed the desire which was in their heart, and Himself giveth them an excuse for questioning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the, “Whither goest Thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Peter used from a very loving affection, Thomas from cowardice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Lord, we know not whither Thou goest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The place,” he saith, “we know not, and how shall we know the way leading thither?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe with what submissiveness he speaks; he saith not, “tell us the place,” but, “we know not whither Thou goest”; for all had long yearned to hear this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If the Jews questioned among themselves when they heard (of His departure), although desirous to be rid of Him, much more would those desire to learn, who wished never to be separated from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They feared therefore to ask Him, but yet they asked Him, from their great love and anxiety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life; no man cometh unto the Father, but by Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then, when He was asked by Peter, ‘Whither goest Thou,’ did He not say directly, ‘I go to the Father, but ye cannot come now’?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did He put in a circuit of so many words, placing together questions and answers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With good reason He told not this to the Jews; but why not to these?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He had indeed said both to these and to the Jews, that He came forth from God, and was going to God, now He saith the same thing more clearly than before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, to the Jews He spake not so clearly; for had He said, “Ye cannot come to the Father but by Me,” they would straightway have deemed the matter mere boasting; but now by concealing this, He threw them into perplexity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But why,” saith some one, “did He speak thus both to the disciples and to Peter?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He knew his great forwardness, and that he would by reason of this the more press on and trouble Him; in order therefore to lead him away, He hideth the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having then succeeded in what He wished by the obscurity and by veiling His speech, He again discloseth the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After saying, “Where I am, no man can come,” He addeth, “In My Father’s house are many mansions”; and again, “No man cometh to the Father but by Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He would not tell them at first, in order not to throw them into greater despondency, but, now that He hath soothed them, He telleth them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by Peter’s rebuke He cast out much of their despondency; and dreading lest they should be addressed in the same way, they were the more restrained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the Way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is the proof of the, “No man cometh to the Father but by Me”; and, “the Truth, and the Life,” of this, “that these things shall surely be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There is then no falsehood with Me, if I am ‘the Truth’; if I am ‘Life’ also, not even death shall be able to hinder you from coming to Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides; if I am ‘the Way,’ ye will need none to lead you by the hand; if I am also ‘the Truth,’ My words are no falsehoods; if I am also ‘Life,’ though ye die ye shall obtain what I have told you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now His being “the Way,” they both understood and allowed, but the rest they knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They did not indeed venture to say what they knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still they gained great consolation from His being “the Way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If,” saith He, “I have sole authority to bring to the Father, ye shall surely come thither; for neither is it possible to come by any other way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But by saying before, “No man can come to Me except the Father draw him”; and again, “If I be lifted up from the earth, I shall draw all men unto Me” ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 32 ); and again, “No man cometh to the Father but by Me” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ); He showeth Himself equal to Him who begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how after saying, “Whither I go ye know, and the way ye know,” hath He added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye had known Me, ye should have known My Father also; and from henceforth ye know Him, and have seen Him”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He doth not contradict Himself; they knew Him indeed, but not so as they ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God they knew, but the Father not yet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For afterwards, the Spirit having come upon them wrought in them all knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Had ye known My Essence and My Dignity, ye would have known that of the Father also; and henceforth ye shall know Him, and have seen Him,” (the one belonging to the future, the other to the present,) that is, “by Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “sight,” He meaneth knowledge by intellectual perception.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those who are seen we may see and not know; but those who are known we cannot know and not know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “and ye have seen Him”; just as it saith, “was seen also of Angels.” ( 1 Tim. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the very Essence was not seen; yet it saith that He “was seen,” that is, as far as it was possible for them to see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These words are used, that thou mayest learn that the man who hath seen Him knoweth Him who begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they beheld Him not in His unveiled Essence, but clothed with flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He is wont elsewhere to put “sight” for “knowledge”; as when He saith, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “pure,” He meaneth not those who are free from fornication only, but from all sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For every sin brings filth upon the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then use every means to wipe off the filthiness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But first the font cleanseth, afterwards other ways also, many and of all kinds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God, being merciful, hath even after this given to us various ways of reconciliation, of all which the first is that by alms-doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “By alms-deeds,” it saith, “and deeds of faith sins are cleansed away.” ( Ecclus. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By alms-doing I do not mean that which is maintained by injustice, for this is not alms-doing, but savageness and inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What profits it to strip one man and clothe another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we ought to begin the action with mercy, but this is inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we give away everything that we have got from other people, it is no gain to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this Zacchæus shows, who on that occasion said, that he propitiated God by giving four times as much as he had taken. ( Luke xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we, when we plunder unboundedly, and give but little, think that we make God propitious, whereas we do rather exasperate Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, if thou shouldest drag a dead and rotten ass from the waysides and lanes, and bring it to the altar, would not all stone thee as accursed and polluted?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Well then, if I prove that a sacrifice procured by plunder is more polluted than this, what defense shall we obtain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us suppose that some article has been obtained by plunder, is it not of fouler scent than a dead ass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou learn how great is the rottenness of sin?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear the Prophet saying, “My wounds stank, and were corrupt.” ( Ps. xxxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And dost thou in words entreat God to forget thy misdeeds, and dost thou by what thou thyself doest, robbing and grasping, and placing thy sin upon the altar, cause Him to remember them continually?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now, this is not the only sin, but there is one more grievous than this, that thou defilest the souls of the saints.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the altar is but a stone, and is consecrated, but they ever bear with them Christ Himself; and darest thou to send thither any of such impurity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “No,” saith one, “not the same money, but other.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Mockery this, and trifling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowest thou not, that if one drop of injustice fall on a great quantity of wealth, the whole is defiled?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And just as a man by casting dung into a pure fountain makes it all unclean, so also in the case of riches, anything ill-gotten entering in makes them to be tainted with the ill savor from itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then we wash our hands when we enter into church, but our hearts not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, do our hands send forth a voice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is the soul that utters the words: to that God looketh; cleanness of the body is of no use, while that is defiled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What profits it, if thou wipe clean thine outward hands, while thou hast those within impure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the terrible thing and that which subverts all good is this, that while we are fearful about trifles, we care not for important matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To pray with unwashed hands is a matter indifferent; but to do it with an unwashed mind, this is the extreme of all evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear what was said to the Jews who busied themselves about such outward impurities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wash thine heart from wickedness, how long shall there be in thee thoughts of thy labors?” ( Jer. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us also wash ourselves, not with mire, but with fair water, with alms-doing, not with covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First get free from rapine, and then show forth alms-deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us “decline from evil, and do good.” ( Ps. xxxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Stay thy hands from covetousness, and so bring them to alms-giving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if with the same hands we strip one set of persons, though we may not clothe the others with what has been taken from them, yet we shall not thus escape punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that which is the groundwork of the propitiation is made the groundwork of all wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Better not show mercy, than show it thus; since for Cain also it had been better not to have brought his offering at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if he who brought too little angered God, when one gives what is another’s, how shall not he anger Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I commanded thee,” He will say, “not to steal, and honorest thou Me from that thou hast stolen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What thinkest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That I am pleased with these things?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then shall He say to thee, “Thou thoughtest wickedly that I am even such an one as thyself; I will rebuke thee, and set before thy face thy sins.” ( Ps. l.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But may it not come to pass that any one of us hear this voice, but having wrought pure alms-deeds, and having our lamps burning, so may we enter into the bride-chamber by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom with the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory for ever and ever.
